pubmed_links: [Baldwin et al. (1994) Mutations in PAX3 associated with Waardenburg syndrome type I](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/8019556/); [Hoth et al. (1993) Mutations in the paired domain of the human PAX3 gene cause Klein-Waardenburg syndrome (WS-III) as well as Waardenburg syndrome type I (WS-I)](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/8447316/)
summary: PAX3 encodes a paired-box transcription factor causally implicated in Waardenburg syndrome types 1 and 3, a congenital disorder that can include sensorineural hearing loss and neurological features; PAX3 plays a broad role in neural crest and neural tube development but has limited direct evidence for classic neuropsychiatric conditions. (novel candidate)
